title: IT operator
description:
We are seeking an IT Operations Engineer with 4–5 years of hands-on experience in production IT operations, change management, risk assessment, security compliance, and AWS cloud environments.
This role is focused on operational excellence, system stability, and secure change execution—not software development.

The ideal candidate is clear-minded, calm under pressure, operationally mature, and capable of making sound decisions in non-SOP scenarios.

Key Responsibilities

Change Management

  • Execute and govern production changes, ensuring adherence to Change Management policies to minimize risk and downtime.

  • Prepare and review change plans, impact assessments, rollback procedures, and approvals.

  • Perform hands-on production changes, including UI-based, command-line, and pipeline-driven executions.

Risk Assessment & Compliance

  • Conduct regular risk assessments across infrastructure and applications.

  • Ensure systems comply with IT security policies and compliance frameworks (ISO 27001, GDPR, internal controls).

  • Support internal and external audits with proper evidence and documentation.

Security & Incident Management

  • Enforce IT security controls and operational security best practices.

  • Support security incident response, investigation, and remediation activities.

  • Work with privileged access tools (e.g., CyberArk) and secure access platforms.

AWS Cloud Operations

  • Operate and support AWS environments, including EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda, CloudWatch, and IAM.

  • Monitor system health, availability, and performance across cloud resources.

  • Support infrastructure automation and deployment activities using Terraform or CloudFormation (operational execution and monitoring).

Monitoring & Optimization

  • Monitor systems, applications, and cloud resources to ensure high availability and performance.

  • Identify operational risks and optimization opportunities.

  • Respond to alerts and incidents in a timely and structured manner.

Collaboration & Communication

  • Work closely with cross-functional teams (Security, Infrastructure, DevOps, Application Support).

  • Communicate clearly during incidents and changes, providing status updates and impact assessments.

  • Maintain accurate operational documentation, runbooks, and SOPs.

Required Skills & Experience

  • 4–5 years of experience in IT Operations / IT Support / System Operations roles.

  • Strong hands-on experience with production change execution.

  • Solid understanding of Change Management, Risk Management, and IT Security Compliance.

  • Hands-on experience with AWS services (EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda, CloudWatch).

  • Familiarity with ITIL / ITSM frameworks.

  • Comfortable using Citrix, Jenkins pipelines, CyberArk, and UI or command-line–based run commands as an operational user.

  • Strong troubleshooting, incident handling, and decision-making skills.

  • Clear communicator with good judgment in ambiguous situations.

Preferred Qualifications

  • AWS Certifications (SysOps Administrator, Solutions Architect, or DevOps Engineer).

  • Experience with automation tools such as Terraform, Ansible, or similar.

  • Familiarity with security frameworks (NIST, SOC 2).

    Disclaimer: The company is committed to ensuring the privacy and security of your information. By submitting this form, you consent to the collection, processing, and retention of the information you provide. The data collected (which may include your contact details, educational background, work experience and skills) will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating your qualifications for the position you're applying for. Your data will be stored securely and retained for the duration necessary to fulfill our hiring process. If you are not selected for the position, your data will be kept on file for a limited period in case future opportunities arise. You have the right to access, correct, or delete your data at any time by contacting us at Quess Singapore | A Leading Staffing Services Provider in Singapore (quesscorp.sg)

    This is in partnership with the Employment and Employability Institute Pte Ltd (“e2i”).

    e2i is the empowering network for workers and employers seeking employment and employability solutions. e2i serves as a bridge between workers and employers, connecting with workers to offer job security through job-matching, career guidance and skills upgrading services, and partnering employers to address their manpower needs through recruitment, training, and job redesign solutions. e2i is a tripartite initiative of the National Trades Union Congress set up to support nation-wide manpower and skills upgrading initiatives. By applying for this role, you consent to Quesscorp Singapore’s PDPA and e2i’s PDPA
